intro: Pelexia is a genus of orchids that are commonly known as "Snake Orchids" due to their long, slender stems and leaves that resemble snakes. The health benefits of Pelexia include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pelexia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pelexia is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as to aid in digestion and relieve stress.
